message:
feat(demo): polish web demo visual system and shared SVG effects

Refine `asupersync_web_demo.html` so the browser demo reads less like a rough
prototype and more like a deliberate presentation surface for the runtime's
concepts.

This commit is purely UI/demo polish. It does not change the underlying
runtime semantics, but it improves the shared visual language used across the
demo's exhibits.

Changes included here:

- introduce a small elevation token system (`--elevation-0..3`) so cards,
  consoles, visualizations, and outcome tiles share consistent depth instead
  of each component inventing its own box-shadow
- introduce shared timing/easing tokens (`--ease-stripe`, `--dur-fast`,
  `--dur-normal`, `--dur-slow`) and apply them across hover, reveal, and
  motion states for more coherent interaction behavior
- strengthen hover/focus treatments on cards, code blocks, consoles,
  visualization panes, task lanes, and outcome cards so the interface has a
  clearer active/hover hierarchy
- add a global `::selection` treatment and slightly richer backdrop-filter/
  saturation settings for the glass surfaces
- inject shared SVG filter and marker definitions (`glow`, `glow-strong`, and
  arrow markers) near the top of the document so multiple exhibits can reuse
  the same visual primitives instead of duplicating them ad hoc
- update the region-tree task rendering to apply the shared glow filter to
  live task nodes, making active execution state more legible at a glance

The goal of this commit is presentation quality and consistency: better depth,
better motion, reusable SVG effects, and clearer visual emphasis across the
demo's many interactive exhibits.
